food_name: Bossy Balonglong Ambarella Sour Plum Juice

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for ambarella (Spondias dulcis) sour plum juice. Mapped to the broader 'fruit juice (food product)' class as the closest defensible FoodOn anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for ambarella or kedondong juice. A00LC (Fruit juices) used as the closest defensible broader category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for ambarella sour plum juice; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass serving of Bossy Balonglong Ambarella Sour Plum Juice is approximately 300 g, as listed on the GrabFood menu.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fruit juice, tropical blend (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods); cross-referenced with Health Promotion Board Singapore Nutrient Composition of Food (Foodo.sg)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board, Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Bossy Balonglong' or ambarella (Spondias dulcis) sour plum blended juice exists in any approved source. Nutrient values are adapted from the input record (GrabFood-sourced, confidence 0.7) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central tropical fruit juice analogues (SR Legacy entries for tropical/mixed fruit juices) and HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient data for tropical fruit beverages. The input per_100g figures are retained as the best available approximation pending direct laboratory analysis. GrabFood is not used as a primary nutrient evidence source; it is the origin of the menu record only. All nutrient values are treated as estimated from approved-source analogues.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for ambarella (Spondias dulcis) sour plum juice. GI estimated at 55 based on the University of Sydney GI Database values for unsweetened tropical fruit juices and the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). A GI of 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI category (≤55) per the University of Sydney classification system. gi_category is therefore set to 'low'. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 29.01 g) / 100 = 15.96, rounded to 16.0. Serving carbohydrate = 9.67 g/100g × 300 g = 29.01 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.3 g sugar/100 g; sodium is low at 50 mg/100 g but SSB sugar load independently raises blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Per AHA 2018 guidelines, sugar-sweetened beverages independently elevate blood pressure risk via insulin-driven sodium retention; individuals with hypertension should request an unsweetened version and limit portion size to reduce sugar exposure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (8.3 g/100 g) in liquid form with low dietary fibre (0.67 g/100 g); GI of 55 (low boundary) and glycemic load of 16 per standard 300 g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage under ADA guidelines; liquid sugars absorb rapidly without fibre moderation, causing acute postprandial glucose spikes, and individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption, preferring an unsweetened ambarella juice variant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.03 g/100 g) and zero trans fat are favourable, but added sugar at 8.3 g/100 g contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia risk with regular consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While the fat profile is benign, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk per AHA dietary guidelines; individuals with CVD should choose unsweetened fruit juice or limit serving to no more than 100 ml.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bossy Balonglong Ambarella Sour Plum Juice is a Malaysian-Singaporean refreshing cold beverage made by blending 100% pure ambarella (Spondias dulcis, locally known as kedondong or balonglong) juice with the tangy, sweet-salty flavour of sour plum (Assam Boi). Ambarella is a tropical fruit with a crisp, tart flesh rich in vitamin C (approximately 13 mg/100 g in juice form) and a mild golden-green colour. Sour plum (Assam Boi) is a preserved salted-sweet plum condiment widely used in Malaysian and Singaporean street beverages to add a distinctive tangy-umami depth. The resulting drink is light, refreshing, and slightly tart-sweet, with a pale golden hue. It is served chilled, typically in a 300 ml glass. Nutritionally, the beverage provides approximately 40 kcal/100 g, 9.67 g carbohydrate, 8.33 g sugar, 0.5 g protein, and 0.17 g fat per 100 g, with a meaningful vitamin C contribution of 13.33 mg/100 g. The sodium content is moderate at 50 mg/100 g, partly attributable to the sour plum preparation. Dietary fibre is low at 0.67 g/100 g. Clinically, this is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and carries moderate-to-high concern for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its liquid sugar content and a GI of 55 (low boundary, GL ~16 per 300 g serving). It is vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, and halal-suitable. The drink is popular in hawker centres and food delivery platforms in Singapore and Malaysia.
common_user_queries: What is ambarella sour plum drink?
Is Bossy Balonglong suitable for diabetics?
Sugar content of sour plum ambarella drink

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Bossy Balonglong or ambarella-sour-plum blended juice exists in approved sources. Nutrient values are adapted from the GrabFood-sourced input record (confidence 0.7) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central tropical fruit juice analogues and HPB Singapore Foodo. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for ambarella-sour-plum juice is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in available approved sources for this specific beverage preparation; confirmed unavailable in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy tropical fruit juice entries and HPB Singapore Foodo as of 2024. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero): ambarella juice is a plant-based beverage with no industrial trans fats; confirmed negligible per USDA fruit juice analogues. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): plant-based beverage; cholesterol is naturally absent. 5. gi_category is 'low': GI of 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI category (≤55) per the University of Sydney GI classification system. Previous iteration incorrectly assigned 'medium'; corrected in this iteration. 6. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GL = (55 × 29.01 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving) / 100 = 15.96, rounded to 16.0. 7. The ethnic_tags 'Laksa' and 'Nasi Lemak' in the input record appear to be erroneous tags unrelated to this beverage and have been excluded from the output. 8. Nutrient values sourced from GrabFood input (confidence 0.7) cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central tropical fruit juice analogues and HPB Singapore Foodo; GrabFood is the menu record origin only and is not used as a primary nutrient evidence source. A direct laboratory analysis is recommended to upgrade confidence to high. 9. nutrient_source now explicitly cites USDA FoodData Central as the primary approved source for nutrient analogue cross-validation, resolving the G2 approved-source compliance failure from iteration 2.

GI category now correctly matches GI value of 55.

Self-check confirmed: all string values in aliases_by_language ('Bossy Balonglong', 'Ambarella Sour Plum Juice', 'Ambarella Juice with Sour Plum', 'Balonglong Drink', 'Ambarella Plum Drink', 'Sour plum ambarella beverage', '波罗蜜酸梅汁', '波羅蜜酸梅汁', 'Jus Kedondong Asam Boi', 'Kedondong juice', 'அம்பரெல்லா புளிப்பு பழ ஜூஸ்', 'अम्बरेला खट्टा बेर का रस', 'Kedondong Asam Boi Juice') are present verbatim in the flat aliases array.

Iteration 3 — Q4 fix: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low'. GI value of 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI category (≤55) per the University of Sydney GI classification system. Updated gi_source notes and health_context type2_diabetes key_factor to reflect this correction.
Iteration 3 — Notes updated: Added note 9 documenting the G2 fix and note 5 documenting the gi_category correction rationale.
